Details: Ultraviolet (UV) irradiation with ozone is effective for mevinphos removal. One study investigated degradation of mevinphos (based on COD reduction) at two pH levels (6.5 and 9.0) using a UV dose of 3 mW/cm2 combined with an ozone dose of 400 mg/hour/liter solution [1654]. The results showed 76.4 and 92.8 percent of COD reduction at pH 6.5 and 9.0, respectively, with an approximate UV contact time of three hours [1654].
